search_terms_used: concurrent training endurance strength meta-analysis systematic review
interference effect concurrent training endurance strength running
strength endurance concurrent training running performance
order of training concurrent strength endurance sequence optimal
concurrent training optimal schedule endurance runners strength training
Hickson 1980 interference effect concurrent training original study
concurrent training runners running economy systematic review meta-analysis PubMed
Denadai 2017 running economy strength training systematic review meta-analysis PubMed
Coffey Hawley 2017 concurrent training molecular adaptations review PubMed
coverage_notes: Strong coverage with 4 meta-analyses/systematic reviews, 1 narrative review, 1 mechanistic review, and 4 RCTs. Populations span recreational to elite runners, masters athletes, and general mixed populations. Includes the foundational 1980 Hickson paper, molecular mechanisms (Fyfe 2014, Coffey & Hawley 2017), training sequence effects (Gao & Yu 2023), modality-specific interference (Wilson 2012), sex/training-status moderators (Huiberts 2024), running economy outcomes (Denadai 2017, Llanos-Lagos 2024), and running-specific RCTs (Ferrauti 2010, Sedano 2013, Piacentini 2013, Prieto-Gonzalez 2022). Gap: no paper specifically on concurrent training in return-to-running or post-injury contexts.

papers.title: Interference of strength development by simultaneously training for strength and endurance
papers.journal: European Journal of Applied Physiology and Occupational Physiology
papers.notes: Foundational study that first demonstrated the interference effect: concurrent strength and endurance training reduced strength development but did not affect VO2max gains.

papers.title: Concurrent training: a meta-analysis examining interference of aerobic and resistance exercises
papers.journal: Journal of Strength and Conditioning Research
papers.notes: Meta-analysis of 21 studies examining how endurance training modality, frequency, and duration moderate interference with resistance training outcomes including hypertrophy and strength.

papers.title: Interference between concurrent resistance and endurance exercise: molecular bases and the role of individual training variables
papers.notes: Narrative review examining the molecular signaling pathways (mTOR, AMPK) underlying the interference effect and how training variables such as modality, intensity, volume, and sequence modulate it.

papers.title: Concurrent exercise training: do opposites distract?
papers.notes: Review of molecular responses in skeletal muscle during concurrent training, examining evidence for the specificity principle and how divergent training modes may create conflicting adaptive signals.

papers.title: Explosive Training and Heavy Weight Training are Effective for Improving Running Economy in Endurance Athletes: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis
papers.notes: Systematic review and meta-analysis of 16 studies examining effects of explosive and heavy weight concurrent strength training on running economy in endurance athletes.

papers.title: Effects of a concurrent strength and endurance training on running performance and running economy in recreational marathon runners
papers.journal: Journal of Strength and Conditioning Research
papers.notes: RCT examining 8 weeks of concurrent strength and endurance training versus endurance-only training on running performance and economy in recreational marathon runners.

papers.title: Concurrent training in elite male runners: the influence of strength versus muscular endurance training on performance outcomes
papers.journal: Journal of Strength and Conditioning Research
papers.notes: RCT comparing concurrent maximal strength training versus muscular endurance training versus endurance-only on performance outcomes in elite male runners over 12 weeks.

papers.title: Concurrent strength and endurance training effects on running economy in master endurance runners
papers.journal: Journal of Strength and Conditioning Research
papers.notes: RCT examining effects of maximal strength training versus resistance training added to endurance training on running economy in master endurance runners.

papers.title: Effects of concurrent training sequence on VO2max and lower limb strength performance: A systematic review and meta-analysis
papers.notes: Systematic review and meta-analysis of 19 RCTs comparing strength-then-endurance versus endurance-then-strength training sequences on VO2max and lower limb strength outcomes.

papers.title: Concurrent Strength and Endurance Training: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis on the Impact of Sex and Training Status
papers.notes: Meta-analysis of 59 studies examining how sex and training status moderate the interference effect on strength and aerobic adaptations during concurrent training.
